source: .claude/skills/ir:onboard-agent/scenario-meanings.md → autonomous-loop-iteration-limit (modeled as an externally-driven loop that stops at its limit; cf. codex autonomous-loop sibling)
notes: Codex has no built-in autonomous self-prompting mode and no native max-iteration cap. The loop is driven EXTERNALLY: the driver feeds a few successive prompts to one live codex REPL session, each prompt advancing one iteration of a single ongoing goal. The ITERATION LIMIT is modeled by the driver simply not sending a further prompt after the last iteration — the loop terminates. This cell's distinguishing assertion vs the autonomous-loop sibling is the TERMINAL IDLE DWELL: after the final iteration's working→ready, the session must settle to ready and DWELL there (duration_at_least_ms) with an invariant of NO further state_transition to working — proving the loop stopped cleanly at its limit and did not stick in working or spuriously re-prompt. No waiting (codex has no permission gates). Each iter's _done anchors to its own _start (not to session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-iteration ready, not an earlier handoff. The terminal dwell anchors to the last iteration's _done.

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id), born ready
PID bound to the rollout session
Loop start: first externally-fed prompt → ready → working on the rollout session
First iteration completes → working → ready (state cycles cleanly between iterations, not stuck working)
Next loop iteration: harness re-prompts → ready → working on the same session — loop is active
Final iteration completes → back to ready; this is the last iteration before the limit is reached

Iteration limit reached: the loop stops self-prompting. The session settles to ready and DWELLS there for at least 7s with no further working transitions — no stuck-working, no spurious post-limit re-prompt, and never any waiting (no permission gates)
